goal: Ship MDViewer: a cross-platform Tauri 2 desktop app that renders .md files, supports threaded comments anchored to selected text, and exchanges document plus comments via files alone — delivered in three sequential phases (viewer + comments, editing + reattachment, CRDT collaboration + distribution).
architecture: Tauri 2 application with a Rust backend (markdown parsing via pulldown-cmark with data-src-offset annotations, syntect highlighting, anchor model with W3C TextQuoteSelector + TextPositionSelector, comments store, sidecar IO, settings, file watcher) and a TypeScript frontend (vanilla TS view layer rendering the WebView UI, theme variables, IPC adapters). Phase 1 ships a JSON sidecar (schema_version 1); Phase 3 migrates to Automerge CRDT (schema_version 2) and adds conflict-diff plus per-OS bundling.
tech_stack: Rust (tauri 2, pulldown-cmark, syntect, notify, serde, anyhow, tracing, automerge, diff-match-patch-rs/dissimilar — Phase-2 selection), TypeScript + Vite, Vitest (frontend unit), cargo llvm-cov (Rust coverage), Playwright (E2E), Mermaid (lazy-loaded).

scenarios.name: Open a .md and view it rendered
Set up profile on first launch
Add a comment to a selection
Edit the underlying .md and reattach anchors
Resolve a text conflict on the .md
Share a document and its sidecar
Change settings and see them take effect

phases.name: Phase 1 — Viewer + Local Comments
phases.rationale: Establishes the Tauri scaffold, multi-document workspace, markdown rendering pipeline with offset annotations, anchor storage, threaded comments, JSON sidecar, theme/Settings/profile/recents — all foundations later phases extend.

tasks.name: E2E spec scaffolding (RED) and coverage tooling
tasks.verification: npm run test:e2e 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-red.txt || true; test "$(grep -cE "✖" /tmp/e2e-red.txt)" -ge 10
tasks.done_when: Playwright, Vitest with @vitest/coverage-v8, and cargo llvm-cov are installed; all 10 e2e spec files exist asserting Given/When/Then scenarios; running the suite reports 10 failing specs (no implementation exists).

tasks.name: Tauri 2 scaffold (Rust crate, frontend root, build constants)
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test scaffold && cd .. && npm run build
tasks.done_when: tauri dev launches a window loading src/index.html; build.rs emits MDVIEWER_VERSION and MDVIEWER_COMMIT_HASH constants exported via lib.rs; scaffold integration test asserts the constants are non-empty; npm run build succeeds.

tasks.name: TS-codegen pipeline (ts-rs export bin)
tasks.verification: (cd src-tauri && cargo run --bin export_types) && npm run test -- tests/codegen.test.ts
tasks.done_when: A `cargo run --bin export_types` invocation rewrites `src/types-generated.ts` with `BuildInfo` (the only IPC type that exists in A2). `package.json` gains `npm run gen:types` mapping to that command. `tests/codegen.test.ts` imports `BuildInfo` from `src/types-generated.ts` and asserts the file's mtime ≤ 24h or the test fails (forces regeneration before commit). All later Rust modules (A3-A7) add their public IPC types to `export_types.rs::Types::export_all()`; ipc.ts re-exports the file so the frontend never hand-writes the shapes.

tasks.name: Settings store with shortcuts schema
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test settings
tasks.done_when: Settings struct serializes/deserializes to TOML in app_config_dir; load/save round-trip preserves all fields (profile, appearance, editor, comments, shortcuts table, sync_provider=null, advanced); change-event channel delivers typed events; default values match the design's Settings page.

tasks.name: Recents (MRU) store
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test recents
tasks.done_when: Recents file persists in app_config_dir; push moves an existing path to the top, dedupes; cap of 10 entries; missing paths are pruned on load; tests pass.

tasks.name: Markdown document parser with data-src-offset annotations
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test document
tasks.done_when: render_markdown emits HTML for GFM (tables, task-lists, autolinks); inline text-bearing nodes carry data-src-offset attributes whose start matches the source byte offset; syntect highlights code blocks when enabled and emits raw <pre><code> when disabled; ```mermaid blocks are passed through with class="mermaid" or rendered raw when the toggle is off; round-trip property test confirms every offset attribute resolves back to the original source slice.

tasks.name: Anchor model with exact-match resolve (Phase 1 subset)
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test anchor
tasks.done_when: Anchor type encodes TextQuoteSelector (exact + prefix + suffix) and TextPositionSelector (start/end byte offsets); resolve_anchor returns offsets when exact text matches; returns Orphan when the quote is not found verbatim (fuzzy matching deferred to Phase B); serde round-trip preserves all fields.

tasks.name: Comments store and JSON sidecar IO (schema_version 1)
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test comments --test sidecar
tasks.done_when: Threads/comments/replies CRUD operations work; resolve sets resolved_at + resolved_by; sidecar at <doc>.md.comments.json round-trips with schema_version: 1 header; missing-sidecar load returns empty store; auto-merge mode setting is wired (Always = newest mtime wins; Ask/Manual prompt path leaves a callback for the frontend); change-event channel notifies subscribers.

tasks.name: Workspace tab manager (Rust struct + tests)
tasks.verification: (cd src-tauri && cargo test --test workspace)
tasks.done_when: Workspace exposes new(data_dir), open_document(path, opts) -> OpenOutcome (Document or Conflict), close_tab, activate_tab, list_open_documents, active_tab_id, settings_store, settings_store update accessor, recents_store, comments_for, comments_for_mut, resolve_anchor_for_tab. workspace.rs imports crate::anchor / comments / document / recents / sidecar / settings explicitly. Borrow-checker safe: open_document detaches the existing-tab snapshot before mutating self.active. Two-tabs integration test confirms independent state. No tauri commands here — this task does NOT modify main.rs.

tasks.name: Phase-1 IPC commands and registration test
tasks.verification: (cd src-tauri && cargo test --test ipc_registration && cargo build)
tasks.done_when: main.rs registers all 13 Phase-1 IPC commands plus app_info. Each handler delegates to a Workspace method that already exists per A8a (no new shims). SettingsStore::update is used (no `set` method exists). CommentsStore methods called are list_threads / create_thread(NewThread{..}) returning Thread / post_reply(&id, NewComment{..}) / resolve_thread(&id, by). The open_document IPC handler emits `show-conflict` when Workspace returns OpenOutcome::Conflict. ipc_registration.rs is a pure-Rust integration test that exercises Workspace shapes and serde envelopes (CommentsStore unknown-tab error, SettingsStore::update round-trip, RenderResult/OpenOutcome serde, Anchor JSON round-trip) — it does NOT use tauri::test mocks, since their API surface drifts across Tauri 2.x patches.

tasks.name: Frontend shell, theme, IPC adapter, StartPage, ProfileSetup
tasks.verification: npm run test -- tests/views/Workspace.test.ts tests/views/TabBar.test.ts tests/views/StartPage.test.ts tests/views/ProfileSetup.test.ts
tasks.done_when: main.ts boots, applies the saved theme (Light/Dark/Follow-system), and mounts Workspace; StartPage matches wireframe 01 (recents + Open + Settings); ProfileSetup matches wireframe 02 and persists name+color via set_settings; TabBar matches wireframes 01 and 03 and dispatches activate_tab/close_tab; Vitest jsdom suites assert each view's render contract.

tasks.name: Document, CommentsSidebar, SelectionPopover, ThreadDetail views
tasks.verification: npm run test -- tests/views/Document.test.ts tests/views/CommentsSidebar.test.ts tests/views/SelectionPopover.test.ts tests/views/ThreadDetail.test.ts
tasks.done_when: Document mounts the rendered HTML, lazy-loads Mermaid for ```mermaid blocks (skipped when toggle is off), translates DOM selection into source offsets via data-src-offset, and renders existing-thread highlights from resolve_anchor; SelectionPopover matches wireframe 04 and creates a thread via IPC; CommentsSidebar matches wireframes 05 and respects show-resolved filter; ThreadDetail matches wireframe 06 with reply composer and Resolve button; jsdom unit tests cover each interaction.

tasks.name: Settings view (full panel)
tasks.verification: npm run test -- tests/views/Settings.test.ts
tasks.done_when: Settings page matches wireframe 11 with Profile / Appearance / Editor & viewer / Comments / Shortcuts (read-only) / Advanced / About sections; each control reads/writes via get_settings/set_settings; theme change applies immediately to every visible surface; Sync provider control is rendered disabled with the deferred-feature note; About panel shows MDVIEWER_VERSION and commit hash.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Light Editing and Anchor Reattachment
phases.rationale: Adds the View/Edit toggle, raw textarea editor, IPC-backed save with watcher self-suppression, fuzzy anchor reattachment, file-watcher with external-change behavior, and orphan-comments UX. Builds on Phase A's anchor and document modules.

tasks.name: Diff-match-patch crate selection and fuzzy reattachment
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test anchor && cargo bench --bench anchor_bench -- --quick
tasks.done_when: diff-match-patch-rs is added to Cargo.toml after benchmarking against dissimilar on representative documents (decision recorded in src-tauri/src/anchor.rs module-doc comment with the latency numbers); resolve_anchor falls back to fuzzy match when the exact quote is missing, scores results against the configured reattachment_confidence threshold (read from settings), and returns Orphan below threshold; property tests over insertion/deletion/replacement edits assert correct reattachment vs orphan classification; Unicode boundary regression test passes.

tasks.name: File watcher with external-change behavior
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test watcher
tasks.done_when: notify-based watcher tracks each open .md plus its sidecar; external changes emit a typed event whose payload honors the Ask/Reload/Ignore setting; external sidecar changes route through sidecar.rs with the user's auto-merge policy; integration tests cover all three modes plus the unsaved-edits override (which always asks regardless of setting).

tasks.name: save_document IPC and watcher self-write suppression
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test document --test watcher
tasks.done_when: save_document(path, contents) writes bytes atomically and registers (path, content_hash, timestamp) with watcher.rs as a self-originated write; the watcher matches and drops the resulting filesystem event; integration test asserts a save followed by no reload prompt; second integration test asserts an external write to the same file still triggers the event.

tasks.name: Edit view, mode toggle, orphan-comments UX
tasks.verification: npm run test -- tests/views/Edit.test.ts tests/views/OrphanComments.test.ts
tasks.done_when: Edit view matches wireframe 07 with raw textarea, debounced autosave (interval read from settings), word-wrap and show-whitespace toggles; Document toggles between View/Edit; on returning to View, anchors below the configured confidence appear in OrphanComments matching wireframe 09 with Relocate / Keep / Delete actions; jsdom tests assert mode transitions and orphan state.

phases.name: Phase 3 — CRDT Collaboration and Distribution
phases.rationale: Replaces the JSON sidecar with Automerge (schema_version 2) plus migration; adds the conflict-diff UI for divergent .md text; adds the share/export dialog and the migrate-sidecars CLI subcommand; finalizes per-OS bundling. Sequencing keeps CRDT migration before the conflict and share UIs that depend on the new sidecar shape.

tasks.name: Automerge sidecar (schema_version 2) with migration from v1
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test sidecar --test comments
tasks.done_when: automerge crate added; sidecars persist as compact Automerge bytes with schema_version: 2 header; loaders detect schema_version < 2 and convert the JSON document into a fresh Automerge doc preserving thread/comment/reply IDs, then rewrite on next save; merge-order-independence test (apply ops {A,B} vs {B,A}) yields equal end-state; v1-to-v2 round-trip test asserts no thread loss; Auto-merge=Always reload is conflict-free under both-sides-edited fixture.

tasks.name: Conflict diff (Rust hunks + side-by-side view)
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo test --test conflict && cd .. && npm run test -- tests/views/Conflict.test.ts
tasks.done_when: diff produces hunks identifying overlapping ranges between local and incoming .md; conflict.rs emits a structured payload (line-anchored, additions/deletions); Conflict view matches wireframe 08 with hunk-by-hunk Accept Left / Accept Right / Hand-edit controls and a Finish merge button that writes the resolved bytes via save_document.

tasks.name: Share/export dialog and migrate-sidecars CLI subcommand
tasks.verification: cd src-tauri && cargo run -- migrate-sidecars ./fixtures && cd .. && npm run test -- tests/views/ShareDialog.test.ts
tasks.done_when: Share dialog matches wireframe 10 and on Export produces a folder containing <doc>.md and <doc>.md.comments.json (already in v2 format) ready to send; mdviewer migrate-sidecars <dir> walks the directory, loads each .md.comments.json, rewrites schema_version < 2 sidecars in-place, prints a summary; both flows are tested.

tasks.name: Per-OS bundling pipeline (.dmg / .msi / .AppImage / .deb)
tasks.verification: npm run tauri -- build --debug
tasks.done_when: tauri.conf.json declares macOS (.dmg or .app), Windows (.msi or .exe), and Linux (.AppImage and .deb) bundle targets; release.yml builds each on its native runner and uploads artifacts; local tauri build --debug produces an artifact for the host platform; code signing left as a follow-up note in the workflow.

tasks.name: E2E suite green (verification only)
tasks.verification: npm run test:e2e
tasks.done_when: All 10 Playwright specs (open-render, profile-setup, add-comment, reply-resolve, edit-reattach, auto-merge, conflict-diff, share-export, settings-take-effect, theme-switch) pass against a built MDViewer; coverage thresholds (cargo llvm-cov >= 90% on touched modules, npm run test:coverage >= 90% on touched files) are met.
